Company:      LumaNord Lighting GmbH
Product:      NordBeam LED Panel 600 (led_lighting)
Regulation:   RoHS heavy-metal limits review (Pb, Hg, Cd, Cr VI)
Deadline:     2027-07-22 · 391 days remaining

WHAT IS REQUIRED
The Commission has reopened the Annex II review for the four restricted heavy metals. Several lighting and legacy-electronics exemptions for lead and mercury are set to lapse, meaning equipment containing these metals above 0.1% (0.01% for cadmium) must be reformulated or pulled from the EU market.

THE GAP
Product contains restricted substance(s): lead.

WHY THIS APPLIES TO YOU
Category 'led_lighting' EEE sold in EU — RoHS substance restrictions apply.

CONSEQUENCES IF IGNORED
€100k+ fine per market · Market ban with no grace period · Marketplace delisting · Brand damage

ACTIONS REQUIRED (in order)
  1. Identify restricted substances: Pb, Hg, Cd, Cr6+, PBBs, PBDEs, DEHP, DBP, BBP, DIBP (R&D / materials)
  2. Collect material declarations from all component suppliers (Procurement)
  3. Check applicable exemptions (Annex III / IV) for your product category (Compliance manager)
  4. Commission lab testing if supplier declarations insufficient (Compliance manager)
  5. Compile technical documentation (keep 10 years) (Compliance manager)
  6. Issue EU Declaration of Conformity (Authorised representative)
  7. Apply CE marking (Operations)

Company:      MediBand Health AG
Product:      CardioPatch Monitor (medical_wearable)
Regulation:   RoHS heavy-metal limits review (Pb, Hg, Cd, Cr VI)
Deadline:     2027-07-22 · 391 days remaining

WHAT IS REQUIRED
The Commission has reopened the Annex II review for the four restricted heavy metals. Several lighting and legacy-electronics exemptions for lead and mercury are set to lapse, meaning equipment containing these metals above 0.1% (0.01% for cadmium) must be reformulated or pulled from the EU market.

THE GAP
Product contains restricted substance(s): lead.

WHY THIS APPLIES TO YOU
Category 'medical_wearable' EEE sold in EU — RoHS substance restrictions apply.

CONSEQUENCES IF IGNORED
€100k+ fine per market · Market ban with no grace period · Marketplace delisting · Brand damage

ACTIONS REQUIRED (in order)
  1. Identify restricted substances: Pb, Hg, Cd, Cr6+, PBBs, PBDEs, DEHP, DBP, BBP, DIBP (R&D / materials)
  2. Collect material declarations from all component suppliers (Procurement)
  3. Check applicable exemptions (Annex III / IV) for your product category (Compliance manager)
  4. Commission lab testing if supplier declarations insufficient (Compliance manager)
  5. Compile technical documentation (keep 10 years) (Compliance manager)
  6. Issue EU Declaration of Conformity (Authorised representative)
  7. Apply CE marking (Operations)

Source:       https://eur-lex.europa.eu · fetched 2026-05-04

alert.message:
[Critical] [Act within weeks] — Regulatory Radar

Company:      MediBand Health AG
Product:      CardioPatch Monitor (medical_wearable)
Regulation:   RoHS lead exemption renewed for certain medical sensors
Deadline:     2026-09-01 · 67 days remaining

WHAT IS REQUIRED
Exemption permitting lead in solder of specific in-vivo and body-worn medical sensors is renewed for a further term. Affected manufacturers should re-document the exemption in their technical file.

THE GAP
Product contains restricted substance(s): lead.

WHY THIS APPLIES TO YOU
Category 'medical_wearable' EEE sold in EU — RoHS substance restrictions apply.

CONSEQUENCES IF IGNORED
€100k+ fine per market · Market ban with no grace period · Marketplace delisting · Brand damage

ACTIONS REQUIRED (in order)
  1. Identify restricted substances: Pb, Hg, Cd, Cr6+, PBBs, PBDEs, DEHP, DBP, BBP, DIBP (R&D / materials)
  2. Collect material declarations from all component suppliers (Procurement)
  3. Check applicable exemptions (Annex III / IV) for your product category (Compliance manager)
  4. Commission lab testing if supplier declarations insufficient (Compliance manager)
  5. Compile technical documentation (keep 10 years) (Compliance manager)
  6. Issue EU Declaration of Conformity (Authorised representative)
  7. Apply CE marking (Operations)

Source:       https://eur-lex.europa.eu · fetched 2026-05-18
Confidence:   88%

Company:      RideVolt Mobility GmbH
Product:      eBike Battery 504Wh (emobility_battery)
Regulation:   Corrigendum: clarification of battery passport scope
Deadline:     2027-02-18 · 237 days remaining

WHAT IS REQUIRED
A corrigendum clarifies the battery passport scope first announced in REG-26-010; no change to which batteries are covered.

THE GAP
LMT battery sold in the EU with no battery passport / data carrier.

WHY THIS APPLIES TO YOU
Product has a 504Wh lmt battery sold in EU — EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 requires a digital passport for this battery type.

CONSEQUENCES IF IGNORED
€100k+ fine per market · Market ban with no grace period · Marketplace delisting · Brand damage

  1. Confirm battery specifications: type, capacity (Wh), cell chemistry (Product / R&D)
  2. Collect carbon footprint data per kWh across the battery lifecycle (Supply chain)
  3. Collect recycled content data: cobalt, lithium, nickel, lead % (Supply chain)
  4. Complete supply chain due diligence documentation (Legal / procurement)
  5. Register product in EPREL (battery product group) (Compliance manager)
  6. Generate Digital Product Passport record in EPREL (Compliance / IT)
  7. Attach QR code to product and packaging before EU market placement (Operations)
  8. Update technical file and Declaration of Conformity (DoC) (Compliance manager)

Regulation:   Battery passport required for LMT and industrial (>2 kWh) batteries

WHAT IS REQUIRED
From the application date, a digital battery passport is mandatory for light-means-of-transport (LMT) batteries and industrial batteries above 2 kWh, as well as EV batteries. Portable consumer batteries are out of scope of the passport requirement.

THE GAP
INDUSTRIAL battery sold in the EU with no battery passport / data carrier.

  1. Confirm battery specifications: type, capacity (Wh), cell chemistry (Product / R&D)

  3. Collect recycled content data: cobalt, lithium, nickel, lead % (Supply chain)
  4. Complete supply chain due diligence documentation (Legal / procurement)
  5. Register product in EPREL (battery product group) (Compliance manager)
  6. Generate Digital Product Passport record in EPREL (Compliance / IT)
  7. Attach QR code to product and packaging before EU market placement (Operations)
  8. Update technical file and Declaration of Conformity (DoC) (Compliance manager)

INDUSTRIAL battery sold in the EU with no battery passport / data carrier.

WHY THIS APPLIES TO YOU
Product has a 2048Wh industrial battery sold in EU — EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 requires a digital passport for this battery type.

CONSEQUENCES IF IGNORED
€100k+ fine per market · Market ban with no grace period · Marketplace delisting · Brand damage

ACTIONS REQUIRED (in order)
  1. Confirm battery specifications: type, capacity (Wh), cell chemistry (Product / R&D)
  2. Collect carbon footprint data per kWh across the battery lifecycle (Supply chain)
  3. Collect recycled content data: cobalt, lithium, nickel, lead % (Supply chain)
  4. Complete supply chain due diligence documentation (Legal / procurement)
  5. Register product in EPREL (battery product group) (Compliance manager)
  6. Generate Digital Product Passport record in EPREL (Compliance / IT)
  7. Attach QR code to product and packaging before EU market placement (Operations)
  8. Update technical file and Declaration of Conformity (DoC) (Compliance manager)
